BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The weather will be sunny. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In particular below approximately 2500 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The weather conditions fostered a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized and can mostly still be released by large loads. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In these regions less snow than usual is lying.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The weather conditions will facilitate a substantial strengthening of the near-surface layers during the night. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The spring-like weather conditions in the late morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The weather will be sunny. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In particular below approximately 2500 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The weather conditions fostered a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized and can mostly still be released by large loads. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In these regions less snow than usual is lying.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The weather conditions will facilitate a substantial strengthening of the near-surface layers during the night. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The spring-like weather conditions in the late morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: At low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack. Places where surface hoar has been covered with snow are precarious. Weak layers in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. In addition the wind slabs of recent weeks are capable of being triggered still.
Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized and can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: At low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack. Places where surface hoar has been covered with snow are precarious. Weak layers in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. In addition the wind slabs of recent weeks are capable of being triggered still.
Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized and can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible from the late morning, even large ones in isolated cases. In particular on steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.

The older wind slabs can be released in isolated cases at intermediate and high altitudes. These can in some places be released by people and reach medium size.

Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise in the late morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible from the late morning, even large ones in isolated cases. In particular on steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.

The older wind slabs can be released in isolated cases at intermediate and high altitudes. These can in some places be released by people and reach medium size.

Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise in the late morning to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a significant increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On very steep sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place large natural avalanches, capable of reaching a long way in some cases, must be expected in isolated cases.

Steep slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The older wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads,. Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m.

Isolated medium-sized natural moist avalanches are possible in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In the late morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a significant increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On very steep sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place large natural avalanches, capable of reaching a long way in some cases, must be expected in isolated cases.

Steep slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The older wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads,. Mostly the avalanches in these loacations are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m.

Isolated medium-sized natural moist avalanches are possible in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.
